frameworks.name: hierarchical Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: perception as joint inference across feature and category levels of the representational hierarchy

frameworks.name: holistic matching model
frameworks.one_line: method-of-adjustment reports reflect optimal joint match in feature and category

frameworks.name: categorical perception
frameworks.one_line: reframed as a consequence of holistic hierarchical inference rather than a separate mechanism

core_question: Do perceptual reports in method-of-adjustment tasks reflect joint inference across both feature and higher-level category representations?
key_assumptions: perception is Bayesian inference across the full representational hierarchy
reports optimally match test and probe at both feature and category levels
holistic match principle generalizes across feature domains
manipulations: comparison against feature-only matching models on existing datasets
key_findings: holistic matching model accurately predicts subjects' response behavior in orientation matching
model outperforms previous feature-only models both qualitatively and quantitatively
model generalizes to color and provides an alternative account of categorical color perception
interpretations: categorical effects in sensory perception are ubiquitous
categorical effects emerge parsimoniously as optimal behavior over holistic hierarchical representations
